jokes.text: Two and ten, babe.
jokes.setup: Elaine giving detailed car instructions to Puddy
jokes.punchline: She's being overly specific about hand positions on the steering wheel
jokes.explanation: Shows Elaine's controlling nature in a specific, believable way

jokes.text: Jesus is one, Jesus is all, Jesus picks me up when I fall.
jokes.setup: Elaine discovers Puddy's radio presets are all Christian rock
jokes.punchline: The overly simplistic, cheesy Christian rock lyrics
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld setup for the Puddy religious storyline with authentic bad Christian rock

jokes.text: According to our latest quarterly... thing,
jokes.setup: Kruger trying to sound businesslike in a meeting
jokes.punchline: He can't even remember the word 'report'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kruger incompetence - the pause before 'thing' sells his complete cluelessness

jokes.text: Or the black, or whatever the bad one is.
jokes.setup: Kruger trying to discuss financial terms
jokes.punchline: He doesn't know which color represents losses
jokes.explanation: Escalates the Kruger incompetence perfectly - a CEO who doesn't know basic business terminology

jokes.text: I sometimes drop off my rent check having 'forgotten' to sign it.
jokes.setup: George offering business advice about cash flow
jokes.punchline: His solution is a petty scam he uses personally
jokes.explanation: Classic George - taking his personal schemes and presenting them as legitimate business strategy

jokes.text: Hello? I sorry, no here Kruger.
jokes.setup: George suggesting they pretend to be cleaning service
jokes.punchline: His terrible fake accent
jokes.explanation: George's enthusiasm for his own bad ideas is funny, though the accent itself is predictable

jokes.text: Silly voices. Come on, people, let's get real.
jokes.setup: George doing silly voice for business scheme
jokes.punchline: Kruger calls for professionalism despite being completely incompetent
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - the most unprofessional boss lecturing about professionalism

jokes.text: When you hit that high note, you say goodnight and walk off.
jokes.setup: George lamenting his inability to end meetings well
jokes.punchline: Jerry's Vegas performer advice for business meetings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cross-domain analogy that becomes the episode's central running gag

jokes.text: It's not like those real musicians who think they're so cool and hip.
jokes.setup: Jerry defending Christian rock as positive
jokes.punchline: He contrasts them with 'real musicians' as if that's a bad thing
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry contrarian logic, though not his strongest

jokes.text: Dumb and lazy, I understand.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if she prefers dumb/lazy to religious
jokes.punchline: Elaine's matter-of-fact preference for understandable flaws
jokes.explanation: Perfect Elaine logic and delivery - economical, character-specific, and quotable

jokes.text: No, the old switcheroo is: You poison your drink, and then you switch it with the other person's.
jokes.setup: Kramer suggesting the 'old switcheroo' for radio presets
jokes.punchline: Jerry's definition involves murder
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld escalation of a harmless phrase to something sinister

jokes.text: Elaine's gonna do the same thing to Puddy's radio that the radio did to her.
jokes.setup: Debate about what constitutes the 'old switcheroo'
jokes.punchline: Kramer anthropomorphizes the radio as an active agent
jokes.explanation: Typical Kramer weird logic, but not his strongest moment

jokes.text: Since the last one.
jokes.setup: Sophie asks how long they've been together
jokes.punchline: Jerry's vague, commitment-phobic non-answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry relationship evasiveness in minimal words

jokes.text: I needed to move a bureau.
jokes.setup: How did Puddy get back in the picture?
jokes.punchline: Elaine's purely transactional reason for rekindling romance
jokes.explanation: Peak Elaine practicality - reducing romance to furniture moving

jokes.text: Well the better ones.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if medical schools actually hire actors
jokes.punchline: Kramer's confident assertion about medical school quality
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer fake expertise delivered with total confidence

jokes.text: Oh, I knew it was you, I was joking. I'm a comedian.
jokes.setup: Jerry doesn't recognize Sophie's voice on phone
jokes.punchline: His awkward over-explanation and professional credential drop
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Jerry's social awkwardness despite being a professional comedian

jokes.text: They ground the head down to about the size of a softball.
jokes.setup: Discussion of statue restoration problems
jokes.punchline: The absurd visual of a statue with a softball-sized head
jokes.explanation: Good absurd visual, though not character-specific

jokes.text: stick a pumpkin under its arm and change the nameplate to Ichabod Crane?
jokes.setup: Statue head has been ground down to softball size
jokes.punchline: George's creative solution to turn it into Sleepy Hollow character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant creative problem-solving that's both absurd and logical - classic George

jokes.text: Alright! That's it for me. Goodnight everybody.
jokes.setup: George delivers the Ichabod Crane solution
jokes.punchline: Immediately implements Jerry's Vegas advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback execution - George taking the advice literally creates the running gag

jokes.text: Sorry, buddy, this is the Hamlet of diseases.
jokes.setup: Mickey wants to trade disease assignments
jokes.punchline: Kramer comparing bacterial meningitis to Shakespeare's greatest role
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor - treating diseases like prestigious acting roles, perfectly quotable

jokes.text: Severe pain, nausea, delusions-- it's got everything.
jokes.setup: Kramer calling meningitis the Hamlet of diseases
jokes.punchline: He describes symptoms like a Broadway show reviewer
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the theater metaphor - symptoms as selling points

jokes.text: So that old woman-- she's just a liar, right?
jokes.setup: George mentioning he saw Titanic
jokes.punchline: His focus on the narrative unreliability rather than the romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect George take on Titanic - missing the point in a character-appropriate way

jokes.text: A Jesus fish!
jokes.setup: Elaine investigating Puddy's religious beliefs
jokes.punchline: Physical evidence discovered on his bumper
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the religious theme, though fairly straightforward

jokes.text: I got him because he seemed so one-dimensional.
jokes.setup: Elaine disappointed that Puddy is spiritual
jokes.punchline: She admits she chose him specifically for being shallow
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest and funny - Elaine's shallow dating criteria stated plainly

jokes.text: She's not a 'me'. I'm a 'me'.
jokes.setup: Sophie using 'It's me' on the phone
jokes.punchline: Jerry's hierarchical view of who deserves to be a 'me'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry narcissism and social observation - highly quotable and economical

jokes.text: It's like those hip musicians with their complicated shoes!
jokes.setup: Jerry ranting about self-absorbed 'it's me' people
jokes.punchline: Random tangent about musicians' footwear
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry random observation, though not his strongest connection

jokes.text: That seems about right.
jokes.setup: Kramer announces he got gonorrhea
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry deadpan reaction to Kramer's absurd situation

jokes.text: I guess there are no small diseases, only small actors.
jokes.setup: Kramer complaining about being typecast with gonorrhea
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'no small parts, only small actors' theater saying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody that perfectly fits the actor/disease storyline - highly quotable

jokes.text: Alright, that's it for me! Good night everybody.
jokes.setup: George delivers the theater saying parody
jokes.punchline: Another premature Vegas exit attempt
jokes.explanation: Good callback development - George trying the technique inappropriately

jokes.text: I'm gonna make people feel my gonorrhea, and feel the gonorrhea in themselves.
jokes.setup: Kramer deciding to add showmanship to his gonorrhea performance
jokes.punchline: Method acting approach to STD symptoms
jokes.explanation: Absurdist gold - treating gonorrhea like a profound artistic challenge

jokes.text: Our eyes met across the crowded hat store. I a customer, and she a coquettish haberdasher.
jokes.setup: Kramer performing gonorrhea with added showmanship
jokes.punchline: Overwrought romantic backstory involving hat store meeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer over-the-top performance with unexpected vocabulary ('coquettish haberdasher')

jokes.text: Gonorrhea!
jokes.setup: Kramer's elaborate romantic gonorrhea backstory
jokes.punchline: Dramatic climactic announcement of the disease
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax to the overwrought performance - shouting the disease name dramatically

jokes.text: It's a goose that's had the old switcheroo pulled on it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about what a gander is
jokes.punchline: Jerry's circular definition involving their earlier debate
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that creates nonsensical circular logic

jokes.text: I'm not the one going to hell.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking if her not being religious is a problem
jokes.punchline: Puddy's casual dismissal of her eternal damnation
jokes.explanation: Peak Puddy - casually condemning his girlfriend to hell with perfect nonchalance. Brilliant dark comedy.

jokes.text: It's a five-mile-an-hour getaway.
jokes.setup: Kramer suggesting Sophie stole a tractor
jokes.punchline: Jerry pointing out the impracticality of tractor theft
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry logical observation about the absurdity of the crime

jokes.text: She lost her thumbs in a tractor accident and they grafted her big toes on.
jokes.setup: Speculation about Sophie's tractor story
jokes.punchline: Kramer's specific medical procedure knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer medical 'expertise' creating disturbing visual

jokes.text: That seems about right.
jokes.setup: Elaine saying she's going to hell
jokes.punchline: Jerry's same reaction as to Kramer getting gonorrhea
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jerry's consistent deadpan reactions to friends' problems

jokes.text: And a slice of Devil's fruitcake.
jokes.setup: Discussion of hell and coffee
jokes.punchline: Elaine ordering appropriately themed dessert
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but feels forced rather than natural

jokes.text: And you always leave me wanting more.
jokes.setup: Kruger praising George for seeming 'on'
jokes.punchline: Kruger not realizing George is just leaving early
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Kruger interpreting George's avoidance as professional excellence

jokes.text: Three times around, no feet.
jokes.setup: George trying to impress Kruger
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating chair spinning as a skill
jokes.explanation: Typical George showing off with meaningless achievements

jokes.text: I don't believe this. I'm being typecast!
jokes.setup: Kramer gets gonorrhea assignment again
jokes.punchline: Treating disease assignment like Hollywood typecasting
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about Kramer's 'acting career' in medical school

jokes.text: Maybe they should change it to a tractor.
jokes.setup: Chess pieces being outdated (horses)
jokes.punchline: Jerry connecting to the mysterious tractor story
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback but not very funny on its own

jokes.text: Wait, don't go. Let's thumb wrestle.
jokes.setup: Sophie uncomfortable with Jerry's tractor obsession
jokes.punchline: Jerry's desperate attempt to examine her thumbs
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Jerry gets increasingly obsessed

jokes.text: This afternoon I found him with sleep creases on his face.
jokes.setup: George complaining about Kruger disappearing
jokes.punchline: Specific evidence of workplace napping
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail showing Kruger's laziness

jokes.text: Sorry, thou shalt not steal.
jokes.setup: Elaine wants him to grab a newspaper
jokes.punchline: Puddy's selective religious adherence
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Puddy's convenient morality

jokes.text: What do you care. You know where you're going.
jokes.setup: Elaine objects to double standard
jokes.punchline: Puddy's casual reminder of her damnation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Puddy's casual cruelty about hell

jokes.text: With devils and those caves and the ragged clothing! And the heat! My God, the heat!
jokes.setup: Elaine finally exploding about going to hell
jokes.punchline: Her specific catalog of hell's unpleasant features
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Elaine's mounting anxiety about damnation

jokes.text: Gonna be rough.
jokes.setup: Elaine's passionate description of hell's horrors
jokes.punchline: Puddy's casual understatement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Puddy deadpan in response to girlfriend's existential terror

jokes.text: Yeah, that's right!
jokes.setup: Puddy accusing her of stealing his Jesus fish
jokes.punchline: Elaine's defiant admission
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their relationship conflict

jokes.text: Hey! That's my cirrhosis! He's stealing my cirrhosis!
jokes.setup: Kramer performing cirrhosis symptoms
jokes.punchline: Mickey treating disease as intellectual property
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - copyright infringement on disease performance

jokes.text: Well, then it's simple. You're both going to hell.
jokes.setup: Priest hearing they're not married
jokes.punchline: Casual condemnation of both to eternal damnation
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - priest's matter-of-fact damnation ruling

jokes.text: Oh, lighten up. It'll only feel like an eternity.
jokes.setup: Elaine saying she's going to hell
jokes.punchline: Puddy's cruel pun about eternal suffering
jokes.explanation: Peak Puddy cruelty disguised as comfort - brilliant dark wordplay

jokes.text: My boyfriend said I got gonorrhea from riding the tractor in my bathing suit.
jokes.setup: Sophie finally revealing the tractor story
jokes.punchline: Completely innocent explanation involving medical misinformation
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expectations - innocent explanation for the mysterious tractor story

jokes.text: Alright, that's it for me. You've been great. Goodnight everybody.
jokes.setup: Sophie's revelation about tractor gonorrhea
jokes.punchline: George's ultimate Vegas exit from the relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback culmination - George using Vegas advice to exit relationship

jokes.text: K-Uger, that sounds like one of those old-time car horns, huh? K-Uger! K-Uger!
jokes.setup: Building sign missing the 'R' in Kruger
jokes.punchline: Kruger's delighted sound effect demonstration
jokes.explanation: Good Kruger moment - finding joy in company dysfunction

jokes.text: Oh no, you're not going out on a high note with me, Mr. Kruger! It's K-Uger! No! No! Goodnight everybody!
jokes.setup: Kruger trying to exit on his car horn joke
jokes.punchline: George refusing to let him use the Vegas technique and doing it himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - George's possessiveness over his Vegas technique creates final escalation
